description: Tables extracted from the ClickHouse SQL source files. 'table' = primary source table in the script's FROM clause. 'joined_tables' = all other tables referenced via JOIN / subquery in the same script. Classification (masters vs transactions) is based on file location: clickhouse_masters/ -> masters, clickhouse_trans/ -> transactions. unique_key = column aliased as ID / TABLE_ID. where_date_column = date column used to filter rows in the WHERE clause (null if none). table_date_column = column aliased as TABLE_DATE in the SELECT (null if absent). where_value_of_initial_run / where_operator = lower bound of the date filter for the first (un-watermarked) load. where_value_max / where_operator_max = OPTIONAL upper bound that turns the open-ended 'date >= floor' filter into a bounded window 'floor <= date <= ceiling'; needed when where_date_column is a FUTURE (scheduled) date such as APPOINTMENTS.JULIAN_DATE, where an unbounded '>= today' would pull the whole forward booking book. The ceiling is typically a rolling SYSDATE expression (e.g. TO_NUMBER(TO_CHAR(TRUNC(SYSDATE),'J'))) so the window edge advances each run; it applies to the INITIAL filter and the INCREMENTAL new-rows branch.
